title: ipomopsis havardii
intro: Ipomopsis havardii, commonly known as the Scarlet Gilia, is a perennial wildflower native to the mountainous regions of the western United States and Canada. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The therapeutic actions of Ipomopsis havardii are attributed to its bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antispasmodic properties.
